title: calceolaria hispida subsp. hispida
intro: Calceolaria hispida subsp. hispida, also known as the hairy pocketbook or hispid slipper flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Calceolariaceae. It is a perennial herb with a long history of traditional use in medicine, particularly in South America, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's medicinal properties have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced wound healing. In herbal preparations, Calceolaria hispida subsp. hispida is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
